consequence.description: TTM calculation breaks when fiscal year ends mid-quarter causing non-standard quarter boundaries, producing incorrect trailing twelve-month figures that misrepresent actual financial performance and lead to wrong investment decisions

core.action: Verify each 4 selected quarters are consecutive and fall within the same fiscal year; if fiscal year boundary causes non-standard quarters, log warning and return NaN instead of silently calculating with misaligned quarters

consequence.description: Silent TTM calculation with misaligned fiscal quarters produces numerically plausible but semantically incorrect trailing twelve-month figures that misrepresent company financial performance over actual time windows

evidence_refs.summary: cc2e399 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-010

core.when: When implementing data staleness detection logic for SEC filings
core.action: Use 999999 days as the sentinel value when filing_date is None — do not replace with None, 0, or any other numeric value, and do not attempt actual date calculation for missing dates

consequence.description: Using None or 0 for missing filing dates causes staleness calculations to return 0 or fail, making complete data absence indistinguishable from current filings and allowing stale data to be treated as fresh

core.when: When implementing hybrid ranking engine for financial document search
core.action: Weight BM25 scores at 0.8 (80%) and semantic embedding scores at 0.2 (20%) in hybrid ranking — BM25 must dominate to preserve exact term match precision critical for financial queries

consequence.description: Equal weighting or reduced BM25 weight dilutes exact match precision, causing semantically related but financially incorrect documents to rank higher than exact matches, degrading search relevance for regulatory and financial queries

evidence_refs.summary: cc2e399 Derived from BD: BD-064

consequence.description: Over-normalizing CUSIPs or CIKs destroys identifier precision, causing wrong securities to be matched; under-normalizing financial magnitudes requires exact year matches where generalization is desired

evidence_refs.summary: cc2e399 Derived from BD: BD-065

core.action: Use <S>/<C> column position markers as the primary parsing strategy when present; fall back to CUSIP anchor regex parsing only when column markers are absent; never attempt to use CUSIP anchor when column markers are available

core.action: Verify that ROA denominator calculations do not use average-of-period assets (BD-035) when combined with Benford's Law fraud detection (BD-054) — the averaging smooths asset values too uniformly, artificially creating Benford-conforming distributions even for manipulated data
